threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still expect the Tube to run on time. Locals just check their phones and shrug when the line's down.
Just saw a guy in a suit on the 333 talking about how he missed the last train. All while two people in the back were totally asleep with pizza in hand.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a guy in a full tuxedo casually buying döner at 3 AM—now that's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you catch a punk rock concert in an abandoned subway station, right next to a lavish cake shop. The contrasts are wild here.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their calm indifference to the BART delays—while newcomers are busy complaining about it on their phones.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the #19 bus passengers with a ukulele. Not sure if it helped the delays, but it definitely lightened the mood.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all locals eat paella every day. Most of us prefer a simple plat de casa, especially on a chill evening.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they're new by the way they order a café con leche without knowing it’s just 'un café'. Still holding onto their home cup size.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can always spot a newbie by how they stress about the bus schedules; veterans know it’s all about the trains and when to walk.
Last night on the 133, a guy serenaded his mates with Elvis all the way from Brixton to London Bridge. Never thought I'd hear 'Can't Help Falling in Love' over the engine noise.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a saxophonist outside Rewe playing a jazz version of 'Schwarze Augen' while an elderly man danced with his shopping bags. That’s Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever noticed the spontaneous techno parties in the most random backyards? Only in Berlin can you hear thumping bass beneath a laundry line.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they recognize the urgency in ducking under the BART platform awning before the next delayed train hits.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Last week, a guy serenaded us on the 21 bus with acoustic covers while the Muni just sat there, stuck behind a dog on the tracks. Classic SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think we all do the 'morning café' thing. In Gràcia, we’re rushing to grab a croissant and a café amb llet for our 9-to-5.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they’re new when they enthusiastically follow all the tourist maps but still ask locals for directions to La Rambla.
